description: A single-run score difference is declared a win without variance or a significance test, so noise is mistaken for improvement.
body: Failure mode: two versions are compared on one run each, the candidate scores slightly higher, and it ships as better, but the gap is within the run-to-run variance from sampling and data order. Detection signals: no confidence interval; single seed; no significance test; differences smaller than typical run variance treated as real. Do not declare a win from a point estimate. Safer fallback: run multiple seeds or bootstrap the dataset, report a confidence interval, apply a significance test sized to the data, and refuse to call a within-noise difference an improvement.
